prestazioni: Replace the stock 440 Magnum (RB-code) engine with a fully built 496ci stroker version using a forged Eagle 4.25" crankshaft, Manley H-beam connecting rods, and JE 10.5:1 forged pistons. Pair with a Comp Cams XE284H hydraulic roller camshaft (230°/236° duration @ .050", .540"/.562" lift) and ported Edelbrock Performer RPM heads with Ferrea 2.14"/1.81" valves.

Install a Holley Sniper EFI 2x4 throttle body system with a FAST XFI Sportsman ECU for sequential fuel injection, mated to a custom 3" stainless steel exhaust system featuring Flowmaster Super 44 mufflers and ceramic-coated Hooker headers (1.75" primary tubes, 3.5" collectors).

Upgrade the suspension with a Hotchkis TVS Stage 3 kit (front and rear sway bars, subframe connectors, and adjustable poly bushings) paired with QA1 double-adjustable coilovers (12-position rebound, 24-position compression) and Baer 13" Extreme Plus 4-piston front brakes with 12" rear rotors.
estetica: Apply a custom 3-stage paint job using House of Kolor Kandy Apple Red over a silver base, with blacked-out trim and a satin black vinyl roof (replicating the 1968 "Scat Pack" aesthetic).

Install a Rare Parts front-end restoration kit (correct 1968-only grille, headlight buckets, and bumper) with LED Hella 500 series headlights and Cibie Oscar driving lamps mounted in the lower valance.

Fit a full-width rear spoiler from Dynacorn (1968 Charger-specific) and replace the stock steel wheels with 17x9 and 17x11 American Racing Torq-Thrust II wheels (ET 0/ET -12) wrapped in BFGoodrich g-Force Rival S tires (255/45R17 front, 295/40R17 rear).
